title: Epstein files cover-up — Bondi said 'fully released,' her own letter said 99% withheld
narrative: Attorney General Pam Bondi and the DOJ have stated repeatedly that the Epstein files have been "fully released" and that the documents show "no evidence" of a broader elite sex-trafficking network. The administration claims transparency has been achieved and that earlier accusations of a "cover-up" were politically motivated disinformation. DOJ officials told Congress the files were "complete."
telemetry: AG Pam Bondi's own letter to a federal judge (May 2026) admitted DOJ has 'not released 99%' of Epstein files — directly contradicting her public statements on Fox News
UN human rights experts concluded the unreleased materials may constitute 'crimes against humanity'
The 3 million newly released documents include hundreds of mentions of Trump and communications with a figure labeled 'The Duke'
Congressional access to unredacted files was granted only after public outcry; initial batches were significantly redacted
Rep. Khanna (D-CA) stated the files were 'scrubbed to protect elite, powerful men'
The DOJ review into whether files were 'mistakenly withheld' was opened only after the discrepancy became public — not before
analysis: The cover-up is documented in the cover-up's own court filings. Bondi's May letter to the judge is the opposite of what she said publicly on Fox News. The gap between the public 'fully transparent' narrative and the DOJ's internal admission that 99% remains withheld is the story. The UN calling it 'crimes against humanity' is not partisan language — it's the language of international law. The DOJ reviewing its own cover-up after it became public is not accountability; it's liability management.
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: The Attorney General told the public one thing and told the judge the opposite. The 99% figure is from her own court filing. This is not a dispute about interpretation — it's a documented contradiction in official records.
